plainEnglish: At Level AAA, provide a way to look up definitions for words used in an unusual or restricted way—jargon, idioms, technical terms. A glossary, inline definitions, or <dfn> markup helps people who struggle with non-literal or specialized language.
whyItMatters: Users with cognitive disabilities, or anyone unfamiliar with the domain, can't understand jargon and idioms unless definitions are made available.
quickCheck: Confirm jargon, idioms, and specialized terms link to or expose a definition, such as a glossary entry or an inline <dfn>.
commonMistakes: Using domain jargon or idioms with no glossary or inline definitions.
Defining a term only once and never linking later uses back to it.
Assuming all readers share your domain vocabulary.

codeExamples.label: Unusual term linked to a definition
codeExamples.code: The request was idempotent — safe to retry. (definition)

codeExamples.label: Jargon with no definition mechanism
codeExamples.code: The request was idempotent — safe to retry.
